message:
fix(install): do not rm lock file in cleanup — flock relies on inode identity

Removing the lock file in cleanup() would unlink the inode while flock
is held on fd 9. A new process would then create a fresh inode at the
same path, acquire flock on the new inode, and bypass the lock entirely.

flock is automatically released when the fd is closed (process exit),
so explicit cleanup is unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -45,7 +45,10 @@ CHANGELOG_1_0_0="Initial release with basic install/uninstall, dry-run, force re
 # Cleanup and error handling
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 cleanup() {
-    rm -f "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
+    # Lock file is NOT removed here — flock is held on fd 9 and automatically
+    # released when the process exits. Removing the file would allow a new
+    # process to create a different inode and bypass the lock.
+    :
 }
 # Trap moved to main() to allow sourcing for tests
